title: Ministerial Direction No 108 - genuine temporary entrant assessment for Student visa applications
authority: Department of Home Affairs / Ministerial Direction

sections.heading: Purpose of the Direction
sections.text: Ministerial Direction No 108 guides decision makers assessing whether a Student visa or Student Guardian visa applicant genuinely intends to stay in Australia temporarily. It explains that decision makers must assess the applicant in a balanced way rather than by using the listed factors as a simple checklist.

sections.heading: Core factors in assessing genuine temporary entrant
sections.text: The Direction says the decision maker should consider the applicant's circumstances, immigration history, and any other relevant matter. It also states that further information can be requested where closer scrutiny is appropriate, including where there are inconsistencies in the application, concerning immigration history, or a study plan that does not align with previous study or employment.

sections.heading: Home country, Australian circumstances, and course value
sections.text: The Direction lists matters relevant to a student visa assessment, including ties to the home country, reasons for not studying there, economic incentives, ties to Australia, whether the programme is being used to maintain residence, the applicant's knowledge of the course and provider, and whether the proposed course is consistent with education and employment history. These are strong intake questions for refusal matters involving genuine temporary entrant concerns.
